title: What is the Source of all Virtues? | Bhagavad Gita -10.4 to 10.7 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, today the discussion is on the topic, the source of all virtues.
We will be discussing four verses, 10th chapter, verse number 4 to 7,
based on the teachings of His Divine Grace, A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shilpa Prabhupada.
Anyone can help me with the answer, what does A.C. stand for?
A.C. I thought it was air conditioner.
So, let's chant this verse together, fourth verse of the 10th chapter.
Bodher Jnanam Asamoha Shama Satyam Damaha
Intelligence, knowledge, freedom from doubt and delusion, forgiveness, truthfulness, self-control and calmness, pleasure and pain,
birth, death, fear, fearlessness, non-violence, equanimity, satisfaction, austerity, charity, fame and infamy, are created by me alone.
How many of us would like to develop some good qualities?
We all want to be truthful, to be honest, to be clean, to be tolerant, yes, no.
How many of us would like to cultivate a lot of good qualities?
So that people can say, bahut acha admi hain, bahut acha vyakti hain.
So, today we'll get a clue how these qualities can develop in our character, in our personality.
How we can genuinely develop good qualities.
So, we'll go one by one.
So, Prabhupada in the parbhupada mentions that real knowledge means,
So, Prabhupada starts the parbhupada saying that real knowledge means,
So, Srila Prabhupada says, real knowledge, there is some defect in modern education.
There is some defect in modern education.
There is some defect in modern education.
Although we have many, many schools, many, many colleges, many, many, you know, institutes all over the world.
But Prabhupada mentions, where is a department, where is an institution which makes us know more about the real self, which is the soul.
This body, we take so much care of this body, do so much for this body.
And when that soul leaves the body, we cremate that body, we just burn it, we bury it.
So, what makes this body valuable is actually the presence of soul.
Minus soul, this body is a dead body.
So, where is that department of knowledge?
We are studying about the birds, we are studying about all different forms of life,
we are studying so many departments of knowledge are there.
Prabhupada, when he went to MIT, he said, where is the department which studies about the soul?
So, real education is which enables us to discriminate between matter and spirit.
Enables us to realize the self.
Enables us to understand what is the real person within this body.
And Prabhupada says, modern education, unfortunately, in the name of so-called secularism, we have kept this body of knowledge aside.
And actually, if you see, lot of people are confused about religion.
In fact, lot of people fighting in the name of religion.
And one of the reason is that this science, actually it's a science.
Imagine if we were not taught physics, chemistry and maths through well-defined textbooks.
I give this example quite often.
When I say that this presenter or slide mover has atoms, molecules, it has electrons, electrons are moving in their respective orbits.
All of you will agree with this.
Atoms, molecules, subatomic particles through well-defined textbooks.
It was not left to our own speculation or understanding.
Because we studied through certified textbooks and they were consistent in their approach.
Whatever is being taught, we have a standard understanding when it comes to chemistry or maths or physics.
When it comes to spirituality, we all have knowledge in bits and pieces from different sources.
Somebody through Chanda Mama, something from our grandparents, something we watched in television.
And we have all connected few dots and we have our own understanding of who is God, what is the purpose of life, what is religion.
And although we have different religions in the world, there is a purpose why different religions are there.
Because there is some kind of lack of proper understanding, systematic study of this religion.
Invariably there is some kind of friction or fight between different people professing different religion.
Fanatically claiming that my religion is better than your religion.
It is better to keep everything away from our curriculum.
If you were to teach Bhagavad Gita then why not Quran, then why not Bible.
So just keep everything away in the name of secularism.
And so called understanding which is very very profound, which is very very important.
Which is very much required in human form of life.
We are missing that important education.
Asamoha means a quality whereby one is freed of delusion and doubt.
In the beginning of Gita, Arjuna is in delusion.
When he sees that opponents are his own kinsmen, own relatives.
Should I fight? Should I not fight?
He is overcome by, overwhelmed with attachment to his near and dear ones.
All of us also at times in our life will go through this phase where we are full of delusion and doubt.
And just like we see Arjuna when he heard the message of Gita from Krishna.
All his doubt was so to say washed away.
All that bewilderment, all that confusion what he had all subsided.
He was very clear about what he should do.
And he executed what was asked to him to.
So we will come to that.
When one practices the principles of Krishna consciousness.
As taught by the Lord in the Gita.
This delusion and doubts what we have.
We will be able to overcome them.
So Prabhupada explains Satyam does not mean that you should speak things which are always palatable.
Sometimes truth has to be spoken even if it is painful.
Just like sometimes doctors have to give an injection.
Or sometimes a surgery has to be performed.
Likewise a genuine saint, a spiritual master will at times chastise using harsh words.
How many of us have heard, have come across.
Harsh words like fools and rascals in Prabhupada's books.
So Prabhupada has also used harsh words at times.
Can anyone help me with one or two words which Krishna has used in the Gita which are harsh?
So he also uses harsh words.
Then there is a verse where it says,
The knowledge has been overpowered by illusion.
Now why does saintly people at times use harsh words?

And he was very put off when he heard this word rascal first time in Prabhupada's books.
He says, how can Sadhu use this kind of a word?
A Sadhu can be very pleasing.
Speak something which is very palatable to us.
It feels very good to listen to us.
Why a Sadhu at times uses harsh words?
It can hurt us at times.

you know, out of mistake, just fall off.

for, what's the end in, so this dilemma even I had, especially when I went to few countries
and I saw all that, you know, spick and span, neat and clean, people very disciplined and

These shops are, you know, spick and span, the packaging is meticulous, especially this,

the way they, the whole thing is, especially, you know, the cigarettes, the Marlboros and

I said they look so neat.

Every packet says cigarette smoking is injurious to health.
What's there in that wine bottle, the whisky bottle, alcohol?
So what meets the eye does not necessarily define what is good or what is bad.
That bottle looks so neat, so nicely packaged, so nicely placed, so nicely advertised, so
nicely being sold by so nice sales people, who are so well mannered, but what are they
What's going to do when you take that?
So likewise, it requires shastra chakshu.
To decifer what is good, what is bad.

There's an element of spiritual understanding.

we Indians have turned our face away from the Lord, and we're looking towards the west.

in the Gita talks about Manu. I spoke this to Sun God Vivasvan.

is known as Pitamaha, the Grandfather. Krishna is known as Prabhupada. So, these are all the
the father of the grandfather. Brahma was created by Prajapati Brahma, we call it.
Because Prajapati Brahma, he created four sages, Manus and these Saptarishis came from Brahma,
who was created by Lord Krishna and then all this prajani procreation came about.
Who was the first created being? Brahma. And to get knowledge about this creation,
it was complete darkness, what did he do? Tapaha. He heard this word Tapaha.
He meditated, he did this Tapasya, remembering the Lord and all the knowledge about how to create,
He who knows in truth this glory and power of mind, engages in unalloyed devotional service of this,
there is no doubt. So Krishna is saying, he who knows in truth the glory,
that everything is emanating from me, the sages, the Brahma, this four, this thing and the Manus,
and power of mind engages in unalloyed devotional service of this, there is no doubt.
Generally people know that God is great, but they do not know in detail how great God is,
how God is great. Unless one is firmly convinced of the different opulences of the Supreme Lord,
he cannot or he will not have that required inspiration and motivation to engage in devotional service.
So here in the Gita, there is another verse where Krishna says,
aham sarvasya prabhavo, aham means I, sarvasya means everything, prabhavo means source, emanation.
Matta sarvam pravartate, mata means from me, sarvam, everything, pravartate, comes about.
Iti matva, matva means understanding. Iti matva bhajante maam, one who has this understanding that I am the source of everything,
everything is emanating from me. Iti matva bhajante maam, will worship me.
Buddha, Buddha means the enlightened one, Buddha, the word Buddha also, Siddhartha became Buddha, enlightened.
Buddha bhava samanvitah, such a Buddha, Buddha means an enlightened soul, bhava samanvitah means worship me in a disinterested way,
in a mechanical way, no, bhava samanvitah, full of transcendental emotions, feelings, he will worship me, knowing how great the Lord is.
When one understands that everything is emanating from me, he is the supreme controller, he is the cause of all causes,
everything is emanating from him, such a wise man, Buddha bhava samanvitah, will worship me with transcendental emotions, with feelings.
Sometimes we see some great sages, great rishis, when they are worshipping the Lord, tears are flowing from their eyes,
choked with transcendental emotions, here standing on,
their skin, we cannot imitate such emotions, out of love of the Lord.
When one factually knows the opulences of the supreme, there is no alternative but to surrender to him.

Therefore, even in Bhakti, there are two kinds of Bhakti.
Sadhana Bhakti means we are trying to remember the Lord with strict rules and regulations.
That is not backed with Raga.
Raga means attachment or attraction to the Lord.
Raga Nuga Bhakti is driven by attachment and attraction to the Lord.
So initially, all our processes are rules and regulations.
We are doing it because the spiritual master has asked us to do.

which is called the morning Sadhana,
which involves chanting, reading, hearing, all those things.

not because we like doing it,
not because we have attachment to the Lord,
we are dutifully doing it because the spiritual master who is close to Krishna,
who understands the Lord, is asking us to do,
we slowly start developing attachment and attraction to the Lord.
The more we develop attachment and attraction to the Lord,
the remembrance of the Lord throughout the day will become automatic.
